perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party agrees it will not use Confidential Information provided under this Agreement to guide or aid a search and evaluation for purposes of showing information, technical data, trade secrets, or know- how provided hereunder is in the public domain.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party agrees it will use Confidential Information provided under this Agreement to guide or aid a search and evaluation for purposes of showing information, technical data, trade secrets, or know- how provided hereunder is in the public domain.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'not to use' to 'use' directly contradicts the fundamental purpose of a non-disclosure agreement, which is to prevent the recipient from using the confidential information for their own purposes, especially to undermine the Submitting Party's claim to secrecy. The contradiction arises because other sections of the agreement, such as 'Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information,' still emphasize the protection and secrecy of the information, now contradicting this definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information . Receiving Party agrees not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by Submitting Party for its own use or for any purpose except to carry out evaluation of the submission according to the Contest Rules. Receiving Party will not disclose the Confidential Information to third parties or to Receiving Partys employees except employees who are required to have the information in order to carry out the contemplated business. Receiving Party agrees that it will take all reasonable steps to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information in order to prevent the Confidential Information from falling into the public domain or the possession of unauthorized persons.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text stated the Receiving Party will 'not use Confidential Information... to guide or aid a search and evaluation for purposes of showing information...is in the public domain.' The changed text states the Receiving Party 'will use Confidential Information... to guide or aid a search and evaluation for purposes of showing information...is in the public domain.' The contradicted text states 'Receiving Party agrees not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by Submitting Party for its own use or for any purpose except to carry out evaluation of the submission according to the Contest Rules' and 'Receiving Party agrees that it will take all reasonable steps to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information in order to prevent the Confidential Information from falling into the public domain.' The changed text and the contradicted text are directly opposite to each other and the contradiction creates uncertainty and unenforceability, falling under the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party agrees that it will take all reasonable steps to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information in order to prevent the Confidential Information from falling into the public domain or the possession of unauthorized persons.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party agrees that it will take steps to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information in order to prevent the Confidential Information from falling into the public domain or the possession of unauthorized persons.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the word 'all reasonable' creates an in-text contradiction. The agreement now vaguely states that the Receiving Party must take 'steps' without clarifying the extent of those steps. This creates ambiguity when compared to the 'Return of Materials' section, which specifies a concrete action ('disposed of in an appropriate manner to maintain confidentiality'). The lack of 'all reasonable' qualifies the steps being ambiguous and contradicting the other sections which emphasize on maintaining confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Materials . NO materials or documents which have been furnished by Submitting Party to Receiving Party will be returned, but instead will be disposed of in an appropriate manner to maintain confidentiality.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'all reasonable steps,' providing a defined level of effort. The changed text removes 'all reasonable,' weakening the obligation to simply 'steps.' This introduces ambiguity regarding the extent of the required actions. The 'Return of Materials' section specifies a concrete action ('disposed of in an appropriate manner to maintain confidentiality'). The lack of 'all reasonable' in the changed text creates a contradiction with the other section because the changed text doesn't emphasize maintaining confidentiality to the same degree as the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: NO materials or documents which have been furnished by Submitting Party to Receiving Party will be returned, but instead will be disposed of in an appropriate manner to maintain confidentiality.
perturbation.changed_text: Materials or documents which have been furnished by Submitting Party to Receiving Party will be returned.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'NO materials or documents...will be returned, but instead will be disposed of' to 'Materials or documents...will be returned' introduces a direct contradiction. This statement contradicts the intent of confidentiality since Returning of data creates a high chance of disclosure in the submission party end, by creating ambiguity for this and for not disposing of the documents, directly breaking the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party agrees not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by Submitting Party for its own use or for any purpose except to carry out evaluation of the submission according to the Contest Rules. Receiving Party will not disclose the Confidential Information to third parties or to Receiving Partys employees except employees who are required to have the information in order to carry out the contemplated business. Receiving Party agrees that it will take all reasonable steps to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information in order to prevent the Confidential Information from falling into the public domain or the possession of unauthorized persons.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that materials would not be returned to maintain confidentiality. The changed text states that materials will be returned. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which requires the Receiving Party to take all reasonable steps to protect the secrecy of the Confidential Information. Returning the materials introduces a risk of disclosure and is thus a contradiction. This adheres to the Omissions – In Text Contradiction definition, because the original text's intent of confidentiality is directly impacted by the change.

perturbation.original_text: The commitments in this Agreement shall terminate two (2) years following the date of disclosure of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The commitments in this Agreement shall terminate 2 years following the disclosure of information that is deemed confidential.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Confidential Information' to 'information that is deemed confidential', the end date of the agreement now depends on a subjective criteria, of deeming the information as 'confidential', by also removing the definition of 'Confidential Information', from the contract, which in turn creates uncertainty regarding when the agreement actually ends. This removal creates a contradiction with other clauses that clearly define 'Confidential Information' and other aspects related to it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definition . "Confidential Information" means any confidential or proprietary information, clinical study protocols, technical data, trade secrets, or know-how of Submitting Party, including, but not limited to, that which relates to research, clinical studies, products, services, customers, markets, software, developments, inventions, processes, designs, drawings, engineering, business strategies, operations, plants and facilities, marketing or finances. Confidential Information does not include information, technical data or know-how which: i. is generally available to the public prior to its disclosure; or ii. is generally known to Receiving Party prior to the disclosure thereof as evidenced by written and dated material in its possessions; or iii. through no fault of Receiving Party , becomes available to the public after the disclosure thereof; or iv. is disclosed to Receiving Party by a third party having a bona fide right to do so; or v. is approved for release by the written authorization of Submitting Party ; or vii. is developed by Receiving Party completely independent of this Agreement. Confidential Information which is specific as to materials, composition, techniques, articles of manufacture, structure, apparatus, methods or the like shall not be deemed to be in the public domain merely because such information is embraced by more general disclosures in the public domain and any combination of features shall not be deemed to be within the foregoing exceptions merely because individual features are in the public domain, if the combination itself and its principles of operation are not in the public domain.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified termination two years after disclosure of 'Confidential Information,' which is defined in the 'Contradicted Text'. The changed text alters this to 'information that is deemed confidential,' removes the defined term 'Confidential Information', and relies on a subjective criterion of 'deeming' information confidential. This introduces ambiguity regarding the termination date, as it's unclear who 'deems' the information confidential and based on what criteria. Since the 'Contradicted Text' provides a clear definition of 'Confidential Information,' the modified text contradicts this by replacing it with a subjective and undefined standard, creating uncertainty and thus a strong contradiction. This adheres to the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction', as essential language was altered, creating a contradiction with obligations or constraints stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be assigned without consent of Submitting Party . Receiving Party shall not through operations of succession or otherwise place Confidential Information in the hands of a competitor of Submitting Party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may not be assigned without consent of Submitting Party .
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence 'Receiving Party shall not through operations of succession or otherwise place Confidential Information in the hands of a competitor of Submitting Party.' creates an inconsistency because without this clause, the agreement becomes less strict, since the receiving party can now transfer or give Confidential Information to the competitor of Submitting party through succesion of operations. Therefore, this creates ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information . Receiving Party agrees not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by Submitting Party for its own use or for any purpose except to carry out evaluation of the submission according to the Contest Rules. Receiving Party will not disclose the Confidential Information to third parties or to Receiving Partys employees except employees who are required to have the information in order to carry out the contemplated business. Receiving Party agrees that it will take all reasonable steps to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information in order to prevent the Confidential Information from falling into the public domain or the possession of unauthorized persons.
perturbation.justification: The original text prevented the Receiving Party from placing Confidential Information in the hands of a competitor. The changed text removes this restriction. The contradicted text requires the Receiving Party to protect the secrecy of Confidential Information and prevent it from falling into unauthorized hands. By removing the restriction, the Receiving Party can now allow the Confidential Information to be transferred to a competitor through succession of operations, which directly conflicts with the obligation to protect the confidentiality of the information, creating a strong contradiction.
